title: Smoky Citrus Japanese Eggplant Curry
description: A fragrant, low‑calorie Japanese‑style curry featuring smoky grilled eggplant and bright citrus notes, perfect for a daring lunch or dinner.
instructions: Heat a cast‑iron skillet or grill pan over medium‑high heat and brush lightly with oil.
Add the eggplant cubes and sear until they develop a dark, smoky crust, about 5 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add the sliced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add minced garlic, grated ginger, and smoked paprika; cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the mirin, sake, and soy sauce, stirring to deglaze the pan. Let the liquid reduce slightly, about 2 minutes.
Add the cut curry roux cubes, stirring until they dissolve and the mixture thickens, about 4 minutes.
Return the seared eggplant to the pan, tossing to coat in the curry sauce.
Stir in lemon zest, orange zest, and lemon juice, cooking for another 2 minutes until the herbs release their aroma.
Taste and adjust seasoning with a pinch of salt if needed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped cilantro. Enjoy the smoky, citrusy depth of this low‑calorie Japanese eggplant curry.
